subject: Project Management
title: Lean Software Development
description: A micro-lesson that makes Lean Software Development usable.

hook.text: Mary and Tom Poppendieck mapped Toyota's seven wastes directly onto software development.

buildup.text: Partially done work, extra features, handoffs, delays, defects — all waste in software too.

discovery.text: Their book 'Lean Software Development' gave teams a vocabulary to identify and eliminate waste.

twist.text: The hardest waste to spot was 'extra features' — teams loved building things nobody asked for.

climax.text: Lean thinking taught the Agile world that doing less, better, beats doing more.

punchline.text: The best feature is the one you don't build.

quiz.question: What was the hardest form of waste to eliminate in lean software?
options: Extra features — building things nobody asked for
